data.title: 'Worst Selloff in 25 Years,' Says Dan Ives: Software Stocks NOW, CRM, MSFT in Focus
data.description:
Wedbush Securities' top analyst Daniel Ives called the recent software selloff "the worst he's witnessed in 25 years." Fears of AI disruption from Anthropic's new Claude model updates have triggered sharp declines in software stocks. Even so, Ives views the plunge in ServiceNow (NOW), Salesforce (CRM), and Microsoft (MSFT) as a "generational buying opportunity," driven by a wide gap between market prices and intrinsic value.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Ives is a five-star analyst on TipRanks, ranking #636 out of the 12,108 analysts tracked. He has a 53% success rate and an average return per rating of 13.40%.

NOW, CRM, MSFT Lose Billions

Ives argues investors wrongly assume enterprise software will become obsolete in the AI era. The stock price declines in these three stocks have particularly caught Ives' attention. Year-to-date, NOW has plummeted 30%, CRM has cratered 28.4%, and MSFT has declined 17%.

Ives believes that AI will complement existing software models rather than displace them. His view stems from a broader claim about AI integrating with and enhancing current modeling frameworks instead of rendering them obsolete.

Based on current prices, CRM now trades at a forward price-to-earnings (P/E) multiple of 16.1x, about 30% lower than the sector median of 23.2x. This is despite reporting triple-digit growth in Data Cloud and $900 million in AI ARR (annual recurring revenue) for the September quarter.

Meanwhile, ServiceNow has pursued an aggressive acquisition strategy to counter AI threats. Yet, the market is penalizing the cloud security provider amid fears that traditional Software-as-a-Service models will become obsolete.

Even tech giant Microsoft has not been spared from investor anxiety. Investors were disappointed by Microsoft Azure's 38% growth projection, dragging its shares down. The fact remains that demand exists, but Microsoft lacks extra compute capacity, which is all tied up in internal projects. Microsoft prefers to rent AI capacity to Anthropic and OpenAI to earn short-term revenue, even though they could disrupt its business in the long run.

CRM, NOW, MSFT: Which Is the Best Software Stock?

We used the TipRanks Stock Comparison Tool to determine which stock among the three is currently favored by analysts. Currently, analysts have assigned a Strong Buy consensus rating to Microsoft and ServiceNow, with NOW stock offering a higher upside potential of more than 78% over the next 12 months.
data.author: Sheryl Sheth

data.title: After a 24% YTD Drop, Is Opendoor Stock (OPEN) a Bargain Buy Ahead of Q4 Earnings?
data.description:
Opendoor Technologies (OPEN) has faced a difficult start to the year, with the stock down about 24% year-to-date as investors remain concerned about housing market trends and profitability. As the company gets ready to report its first full-quarter results under new CEO Kaz Nejatian on February 19, investors are optimistic on the company's updates around the turnaround progress and early signs of recovery. That said, the stock is best suited for risk-tolerant investors.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

For context, Opendoor Technologies is a digital platform that streamlines the process of buying and selling homes.

All Eyes on Opendoor's Turnaround as Earnings Approach

OPEN stock was one of the meme stocks of the year in 2025. It has come down from those highs, as the company's iBuying model has struggled to perform in a difficult housing market. At the same time, mortgage rates have stayed high even after the Federal Reserve began cutting interest rates, although there are early signs that conditions could start improving.

This creates both challenges and opportunities for Opendoor under the new CEO Nejatian. He has already begun reshaping the company's strategy by reducing costs, increasing the use of AI, and focusing more on transaction volume. Nejatian has also pointed out that management relied too heavily on outside consultants in the past and is now bringing more of that work in-house.

Still, the turnaround may take time. Revenue continues to decline, falling 34% year over year in the third quarter. Inventory dropped from 6,288 homes to 3,319 by the end of the quarter, while home sales fell to 2,568 from 3,165 a year earlier. The company also remains unprofitable.

What's Lies Ahead for Investors?

On Wall Street, analysts expect a Q4 loss of $0.12 per share, compared with a loss of $0.16 a year ago. Meanwhile, revenue is projected to come in at $594.04 million, down from $1.1 billion in the same quarter last year.

Opendoor's new CEO has improved investor sentiment, but significant challenges remain, and consistent profitability is still uncertain. Given the stock's volatility and weak financial outlook, investors may want to proceed cautiously and carefully weigh the risks tied to the company's turnaround.

Is OPEN Stock a Good Buy?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Hold consensus rating on OPEN stock based on two Holds assigned in the past three months. Furthermore, the average OPEN price target of $5.0 per share implies an upside of 12.5%.
data.author: Kirti Tak

data.title: PLTR Stock Jumps from Sell to Buy — Analyst Says 'AI Agents Not a Threat to Palantir'
data.description:
Freedom Capital Markets analyst Almas Almaganbetov issued a rare double upgrade for Palantir Technologies (PLTR) after its robust Q4FY25 results and upbeat guidance. He upgraded PLTR from Sell to Buy, stating that he does not see AI agents as a threat to Palantir's long-term business model. Almaganbetov also set a price target of $170, implying 29.4% upside potential from current levels. PLTR stock gained 1.7% on Friday, following the upgrade.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The big data analytics company reported Q4 earnings per share of $0.25, surpassing the $0.23 consensus. Revenue hit $1.407 billion, up 70% from the prior year and ahead of the $1.34 billion forecast. Commercial revenue surged 137% year-over-year to $507 million, while government revenue grew 66% to $570 million.

Analyst Highlights AIP Growth Story

Almaganbetov noted that Palantir's Q4 results far exceeded both management's guidance and his estimates. The company's outlook for Q1 and FY26 also topped consensus. He noted that this strong forecast signals Palantir management's confidence that demand for AI infrastructure is durable and structural, not cyclical.

Almaganbetov remains particularly impressed by the growth in the company's Artificial Intelligence Platform (AIP), which is driving U.S. market growth by accelerating sales and turning pilots into major contracts. He called the recent sharp pullback "unjustified," given continued hypergrowth and the underlying strength in AIP. He also raised his revenue forecasts for fiscal 2026 and 2027, reiterating that new AI agents pose no long-term risk.

Not all analysts agree. D.A. Davidson analyst Gil Luria maintained his "Hold" rating, viewing PLTR shares as "expensive." Meanwhile, he dismissed concerns from Big Short investor Michael Burry's recent 10,000-page thesis, which raises concerns about the sustainability of the current AI investment cycle. Following his newsletter, PLTR stock plunged 6.7% on February 12.

Is Palantir a Good Stock to Buy?

Wall Street remains divided on Palantir's long-term prospects. On TipRanks, PLTR stock has a Moderate Buy consensus rating based on 11 Buys, six Holds, and two Sell ratings. The average Palantir Technologies price target of $192.38 implies 46.4% upside potential from current levels. Over the past year, PLTR shares have gained 10.3%.
data.author: Sheryl Sheth

data.title: OpenAI Hires OpenClaw Creator Steinberger to Power AI Agent Push
data.description:
OpenAI is doubling down on its push into autonomous AI agents with a key new hire. The company has tapped Peter Steinberger, creator of the open-source AI agent OpenClaw, as part of its efforts to expand its product capabilities. His appointment follows OpenClaw's recent surge in popularity, driven by its ability to function as a personal assistant.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

According to a social media update from OpenAI's CEO Sam Altman, OpenClaw will remain an open-source project under a foundation, with OpenAI continuing to support its development.

In a separate post, Steinberger said he is joining OpenAI to be part of the "frontier of AI research and development" while continuing to build new technologies. He emphasized that keeping OpenClaw open source has always been a priority for him.

OpenAI Strengthens AI Agent Push

The move reflects the rising importance of AI agents. Notably, Altman believes agents will shape the next phase of AI, even though the technology is still in its early stages. As a result, tech companies are now competing to build smarter systems that can complete real-world tasks with greater independence.

OpenClaw, previously known as Clawdbot and Moltbot, has gained a strong following since its launch in November thanks to its ability to operate autonomously. The tool can manage tasks such as clearing email inboxes, booking restaurant reservations, and checking users in for flights. It also integrates with messaging platforms like Meta's (META) WhatsApp and Slack, allowing users to control the agent directly through those apps.

Meanwhile, OpenClaw has also raised concerns about possible security risks if used incorrectly.

data.title: Warner Bros. May Restart Talks with Paramount Skydance after Sweetened Offer
data.description:
Wall Street is in the midst of an intense bidding war for Warner Bros. Discovery (WBD), pitting Paramount Skydance (PSKY) against Netflix (NFLX). According to Bloomberg, the WBD board is considering reopening deal talks with Paramount after it sweetened its offer on February 10.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Paramount has agreed to pay a $650 million ($0.25 per share) quarterly ticking fee if the deal drags past December 31, signaling confidence in quick regulatory approval. It also committed to covering WBD's $2.8 billion breakup fee to Netflix if that deal collapses, plus up to $1.5 billion in WBD's debt refinancing costs.

What Warner Bros. Needs to Do

If WBD indeed restarts talks with Paramount, it must first notify Netflix, which inked an $82.7 billion sale agreement in December. WBD could then try to push Paramount to top its current $30-per-share offer. If WBD's board deems Paramount's improved bid is better, Netflix will get a chance to match it.

Notably, both Paramount and Netflix have indicated willingness to raise their offers. WBD reportedly plans to respond to Paramount's offer and announce its Q4 2025 earnings date early this week, after the Presidents' Day holiday on February 16. Investors are also waiting for the date of WBD's special shareholder vote on the Netflix deal.

The WBD-Netflix deal faces tough D.C. regulatory scrutiny over Netflix's streaming dominance and Trump-era media politics. WBD may also face PR pushback from noisy investors, as seen last week with Ancora Holdings. Ancora built a $200 million stake in WBD and plans to oppose the Netflix deal. Having said that, only 42.3 million shares (under 2% of those outstanding) have been tendered in Paramount's favor so far.

Is Paramount Stock a Good Buy?

Analysts remain cautious on Paramount Skydance's long-term outlook. On TipRanks, PSKY stock has a Moderate Sell consensus rating based on zero Buys, one Hold, and three Sell ratings. The average Paramount Skydance price target of $12.33 implies 19.5% upside potential from current levels. Over the past year, PSKY shares have dropped 8.7%.
data.author: Sheryl Sheth

data.title: The Market's Biggest Test Yet? Nvidia (NVDA) Heads Into Earnings
data.description:
Nvidia (NVDA) will report its fourth-quarter fiscal 2026 results on February 25, and investors are watching closely for what the company says about demand for AI chips. After a year in which its processors helped lift much of the market, traders want to know whether large cloud companies are still spending heavily on AI data centers. The company's comments could affect not only Nvidia's stock but also the mood across the broader tech sector.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Nvidia is the leading supplier of chips used to train and run AI systems. Because of this role, investors often treat its guidance as a signal for broader AI spending, not just a company-specific update.

What Investors Are Watching

Over the past year, Nvidia's results have repeatedly exceeded forecasts, helped by tight supply of AI chips and aggressive spending by large cloud providers.

The upcoming report matters because comparisons are becoming harder and investors want to know whether that pace of growth can continue.

In particular, attention will center on the rollout of Nvidia's next-generation Blackwell platform. Analysts are watching shipment timing, production capacity, and customer demand, as these factors will influence revenue growth over the coming quarters.

Wall Street Expectations from Nvidia

Wall Street expects another strong report, with earnings and revenue projected to rise sharply from a year ago. Data-center products, which include the company's AI accelerators, continue to be the main growth driver.

For Q4, analysts expect Nvidia to post adjusted earnings per share (EPS) of $1.52, up nearly 71% from the year-ago figure of $0.89. Moreover, sales are forecast to jump 67% year-over-year to $65.56 billion.

What the Market Wants to Hear

For this report, investors will pay close attention to management's outlook. They will listen for updates on orders from large cloud customers, spending plans, and whether companies building AI systems are still expanding capacity.

If Nvidia indicates that demand remains strong, it would support the view that the AI build-out is still in its early stages. However, any signs of slower orders or delayed deployments could raise concerns that expectations have moved ahead of reality.

As a result, the report is being watched beyond the company itself. Many investors now see Nvidia as a gauge for AI spending, so its comments could influence sentiment across the wider technology sector.

Is Nvidia a Good Stock to Buy?

On TipRanks, Nvidia stock commands a Strong Buy consensus rating based on 37 Buys, one Hold, and one Sell rating. The average Nvidia price target of $260.38 implies 39.29% upside potential from current levels.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: Nvidia Stock Forecast Ahead of Earnings – Here's What to Expect
data.description:
Nvidia (NVDA) is set to release its fourth-quarter fiscal 2026 results on February 25. With AI GPU demand staying robust, investors will look for confirmation that revenue and margins can support current valuations. The chip giant's results mark a pivotal moment for investors tracking its lead amid booming GPU demand and competitive pressures. With shares trading around $185–$190 recently, Wall Street anticipates robust results that could propel the stock higher or expose valuation risks.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Nvidia dominates the AI infrastructure market with an estimated 80% GPU share through its H100/H200 AI chips and emerging Blackwell platforms. At the same time, Nvidia faces an ongoing battle over China market recovery, Rubin GPU timelines, and hyperscaler spending. Over the past year, NVDA shares have surged nearly 45%.

Expectations from Nvidia

According to Wall Street estimates, Nvidia is expected to post adjusted earnings per share (EPS) of $1.52, up nearly 71% from the year-ago figure of $0.89. Moreover, sales are forecast to jump 67% year-over-year to $65.58 billion.

For full-year fiscal 2026, sales are projected at $213.3 billion and adjusted earnings at $4.69 per share. Data center sales have been a major revenue driver for Nvidia through the first nine months of the fiscal year, totaling $131.4 billion.

Analysts' Views Ahead of Results

Heading into the results, a majority of analysts have reiterated their Buy ratings on NVDA stock. UBS analyst Timothy Arcuri lifted his price target from $235 to $245, implying 34% upside. He noted that the supply chain remains strong and the setup into the next earnings period is favorable, particularly with the GTC developer conference coming up in mid-March.

Arcuri also believes Nvidia will exclude China sales from its guidance since market reopening is still uncertain amid rising domestic chip adoption. Profitability questions persist as peers build AI processors. UBS sees little near-term risk to Nvidia's roughly 75% gross margin and expects management to address competitive concerns during the earnings call and GTC.

Similarly, Goldman Sachs analyst James Schneider maintained a $250 price target ahead of the results, suggesting 36.7% upside. He expects Nvidia's Q4 sales to hit $67.3 billion, implying a $2 billion revenue surprise above the consensus, and a bottom-line beat.

Schneider highlighted drivers that could push Nvidia's stock higher after the results, including stronger-than-expected hyperscalers' capex, positive comments on FY27 data-center sales forecast, a ramp in GPU demand from LLM companies, the competitive advantages of Nvidia's CUDA platform, China demand trends, and progress on Rubin production.

Is Nvidia a Good Stock to Buy?

On TipRanks, Nvidia stock commands a Strong Buy consensus rating based on 37 Buys, one Hold, and one Sell rating. The average Nvidia price target of $260.38 implies 42.4% upside potential from current levels.
data.author: Sheryl Sheth

data.title: The Week That Was, The Week Ahead: Macro and Markets, Feb. 15
data.description:
U.S. stocks ended the week lower as investors moved away from big tech and focused more on safer areas of the market. The S&P 500 (SPX) fell 1.28%, the Nasdaq 100 (NDX) dropped 1.27%, and the Dow (DJIA) slid 1.15%. At the same time, the 10-year Treasury yield eased to about 4.09%, while gold (CM:XAUUSD) jumped above $5,000 and oil (CM:CL) held near $63 a barrel.

data.title: Tesla Stock: Energy Boom vs. EV Bust – Can FSD Drive 2026 Recovery?
data.description:
Shares of electric vehicle (EV) giant Tesla (TSLA) traded around $411-$420 in early 2026, down 7% year-to-date amid EV sales declines. Yet, Tesla's most bullish analyst eyes potential 40%+ upside from energy storage growth and Full Self-Driving (FSD) monetization.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

EV Challenges Weigh Heavy

Tesla's Q4 FY25 deliveries dropped 15% year-over-year to 418,000 units, marking a second annual decline. Fierce competition from Chinese rival BYD (BYDDF) and softening demand in Europe, where registrations fell over 40%, have led to the declines. Leadership shake-ups, including a new global sales head, highlight internal efforts to counter eroding market share and margin-squeezing price wars.

Energy Business Powers Ahead

Energy storage deployments hit records in 2025, positioning Megapack and Powerwall for $12 to $15 billion in revenue by 2027, diversifying beyond vehicles hit by macroeconomic headwinds. This high-margin segment could offset auto weakness, with 2025 revenue at $95 billion despite a 2.9% dip. Investors see it as a stabilizing force amid Tesla's pivot to software-led growth.

FSD and Robotics to the Rescue

Tesla's FSD subscriptions could generate $50-$100 monthly per vehicle, potentially lifting margins to 13%-20% by 2028 if regulatory approvals accelerate. Tesla's $20 billion 2026 capex guidance targets autonomous driving and Optimus robots. CEO Elon Musk plans to end production of premium Model S and Model X at its California factories to reallocate capacity to Optimus.

Analysts' Views on Tesla Stock

Tigress Financial analyst Ivan Feinseth initiated coverage with a Buy rating and $550 price target, implying 31.9% upside potential. He sees Tesla shifting its focus from a pure-play EV maker to a broad AI platform featuring self-driving tech. He noted that growth will come from more FSD subscriptions, robotaxis, and Optimus robots, adding to its strong EV and energy businesses.

Morgan Stanley analyst Andrew Percoco maintained his Hold rating and $415 price target, suggesting that shares are fully valued at current levels. He sees Tesla's solar manufacturing push as a smart long-term bet on geopolitics and data center demand. At full capacity, Tesla Solar could add $20 billion to $50 billion in value ($6 to $14 per share) to its $140 billion energy business ($40 per share). Although not huge on a standalone basis, vertical integration could boost growth.

Is Tesla Stock a Good Buy Right Now?

Analysts remain cautious about Tesla's long-term prospects. On TipRanks, TSLA stock has a Hold consensus rating based on 12 Buys, 11 Holds, and seven Sell ratings. The average Tesla price target of $396.80 implies 4.9% downside potential from current levels. Over the past year, TSLA shares have gained 17%.
data.author: Sheryl Sheth
